title: Case/Care Management * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Case/Care Management * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions 30 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Chronic/Severe Mental Illness Deafness Disabilities Issues Hard of Hearing Older Adults Hispanic/Latino Community Immigrants Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Homeless Veterans Veterans Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness BAKER COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs. Home visitors help families coordinate care for their children, connect with healthcare, community support, and make sure their child’s health team works together. Helps prepare older youth with special health needs for the transition to adulthood. 2200 4th Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Families with children and youth ages 0-21 with chronic health or developmental conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 523-8211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BARBARA ROSS BUILDING CHILDREN’S OUTPATIENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual, family, and group therapy, case management, skills training, and parent education. Helps prepare older youth with special health needs for the transition to adulthood. 2111 8th Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-21 with chronic health or developmental conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 842-3943 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides non-clinical case management services that focus on identifying areas requiring support, and helping individuals navigate and access available community resources. 2611 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Individuals with a serious health condition and a limited life expectancy Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 322-1289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a wide range of services that may include networking and outreach, facilitation of Individual Support Plan (ISP) meetings, assisting in the referral process to community service agencies for supports, providing support to individuals and families in a variety of planning meetings, assisting in finding residential placement and employment services, as well as referrals to support services brokerages. The DD program staff may also review complaints of abuse and immediately take steps to assess further risk. In some cases, protective services may be offered to avoid further harm. 817 SE 13th Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must have the all of the following: -A disability that originated in the brain -The disability must be established prior the age of 22, or in the case of intellectual disability the condition must be determined before 18 years of age. -Must result in significant impairments in at least two of the following areas of daily function: Self-care, communication, cognition, mobility, self direction, capacity of independent living, and/or economic self sufficiency -The impairments must not be primarily related to: substance abuse, mental illness, an emotional disorder, ADHD, a learning disability, or sensory impairment. Impairments must be expected to last indefinitely. Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm, Friday 8am-noon Email (541) 278-4450 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK MARTHA YOUNG FAMILY SERVICE CENTER CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs.
